title: Houses for Rent in Slavic Village, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Slavic Village, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Find houses for rent in Slavic Village (44105), Cleveland. Rent ranges, transit, and Section 8-friendly homes from a local property manager. Book a showing.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent slavic village cleveland
answerBox: Slavic Village, on Cleveland's southeast side in ZIP code 44105, is one of the city's most affordable neighborhoods for renters. Across Rent Finder Cleveland's managed homes and comparable local listings, two- and three-bedroom houses here typically rent from the mid-$700s to about $1,250 a month, and every home we manage in the area accepts Section 8 vouchers.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Slavic Village
body: Slavic Village sits southeast of downtown Cleveland along the Morgana Run Trail, split between the Broadway-Slavic Village and North Broadway areas within **ZIP code 44105**. It's one of the city's larger residential neighborhoods and one of its more affordable for renters looking for a full house rather than an apartment.

Most of the rental housing stock here is early-1900s single-family homes, duplexes, and a smaller number of triplexes with front porches, small yards, and full basements. Rent Finder Cleveland manages voucher-friendly rental homes throughout Slavic Village — every property we manage accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. [See what's available now](/p/book-showing) or [tell our team what you need](/p/book-showing) and we'll follow up.

sections.h2: How much is rent in Slavic Village?
body: Within Rent Finder Cleveland's own managed homes and comparable local listings in the 44105 area, two-bedroom houses generally rent in the mid-$700s to roughly $950 a month, while three-bedroom homes commonly run from about $1,000 to $1,250, depending on condition, size, and whether utilities are included. Slavic Village consistently ranks among the more affordable Cleveland neighborhoods for house rentals — well below both Zumper's July 4, 2026 citywide 2-bedroom median of $1,100 and RentCafe's July 2, 2026 professionally-managed average of $1,818 for a 2-bedroom unit, since most Slavic Village rentals are smaller, owner-managed homes rather than large apartment complexes.

rows: Often with a basement and yard

body: Slavic Village is served by several GCRTA bus routes connecting to downtown Cleveland and Tower City, and the neighborhood is close to I-77 and I-490 for drivers. Morgana Run Trail offers a paved path for walking and biking along a former rail corridor, and the neighborhood's commercial strips along Broadway Avenue and Fleet Avenue provide everyday shopping, groceries, and dining.

items: **ZIP code:** 44105 (one of Cleveland's most-populated rental ZIPs)
**Transit:** Multiple GCRTA bus lines to downtown and Tower City; near I-77 and I-490
**Outdoors:** Morgana Run Trail, Mill Creek Falls nearby
**Everyday needs:** Shopping and dining along Broadway and Fleet Avenues

sections.h2: Section 8 and vouchers in Slavic Village
body: Slavic Village has long been a Cleveland neighborhood where Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) holders find homes. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready, so voucher holders can tour and apply with confidence. Applications for a voucher itself go through the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA), which accepts preliminary applications year-round through its online portal. Keep in mind that Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and the City of Cleveland does not currently have a source-of-income protection ordinance — so it's worth confirming voucher acceptance with any owner before applying. Learn more in our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/).

sections.h2: What to expect from Slavic Village's housing stock
body: Because Slavic Village developed largely in the early 1900s, most homes are wood-frame single-family houses and two-family duplexes on modest lots, many with detached garages or off-street parking pads. Full basements are common, which locally means added storage or utility space rather than finished living area. Renters comparing Slavic Village to nearby Buckeye or Union-Miles will find a similar era and scale of housing stock, though exact block-by-block condition varies — a showing is the best way to see a specific home's layout and updates.

If Slavic Village doesn't have what you need right now, browse our broader [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) hub, or compare nearby neighborhoods like [Buckeye](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-buckeye-cleveland/) and [Hough](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-hough-cleveland/).

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in Slavic Village?
faq.a: Yes. Slavic Village (44105) is one of the more common Cleveland neighborhoods for voucher-friendly rentals. Every home managed by Rent Finder Cleveland accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Book a showing to see what's currently available.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Slavic Village?
faq.a: Two-bedroom houses generally rent from the mid-$700s to about $950 a month, and three-bedroom homes from roughly $1,000 to $1,250. Slavic Village is among Cleveland's more affordable neighborhoods for house rentals, well below the citywide averages reported by Zumper and RentCafe for larger, professionally-managed units.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Slavic Village in?
faq.a: Slavic Village is primarily in ZIP code 44105 on Cleveland's southeast side, covering the Broadway-Slavic Village and North Broadway areas.

faq.q: What kind of houses are available in Slavic Village?
faq.a: Most rental housing in Slavic Village is early-1900s wood-frame single-family homes and two-family duplexes, typically with a full basement and a small yard. Two- and three-bedroom layouts are the most common sizes.

faq.q: How do I get to downtown Cleveland from Slavic Village?
faq.a: Several GCRTA bus routes connect Slavic Village to downtown Cleveland and the Tower City rail hub, and the neighborhood sits close to I-77 and I-490 for drivers heading downtown or toward the southern suburbs.
